marketState: neutral with a bearish longer-term tilt, propped up short-term by hot weather
summary: Front price ($2.915) is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.917), so there is no valuation edge right now. Seasonality is modestly bearish but low-reliability: price is already 11pp below its typical seasonal path, odds of being higher in 1/2/4 weeks are all under 50%, and the typical 4-week move is -3.1%. That bearish seasonal tilt is reinforced by a +184 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average with this week's build surprising +72 Bcf bearish, net-short managed-money positioning, and a bearish trend/death-cross (50<200 EMA) on the 1h/4h/1d/1w technicals. The main counterweight is near-term weather: 1-7D demand is running +24% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range (rangePos 0.93), and 8-16D demand is still +10% above normal, which explains why the curve shows slight front-over-next backwardation (tight prompt market) even as storage stays comfortably in surplus.

longTerm.invalidation: A sustained break of weather demand back above the current hot readings (1-7D/8-16D staying >+10-20% vs 5y) alongside a run of bearish (below-forecast) storage builds would flip the fundamental balance and invalidate the short.

shortTerm.invalidation: 5m/15m technicals are only neutral and 1h/4h/1d trend reads bearish with a 50<200 EMA cross; if the heat-demand readings fade back toward normal or price breaks below $2.80, the prevailing technical downtrend should be respected instead of this weather-driven long.

risks: Weather outlooks can flip quickly, especially the 8-16D window; a cooling shift would remove the main bullish prop and open the door to the storage-surplus-driven downside
